parts.text: My budget is INR 500 for 4 days. I've spent INR 172. Top categories: meals (172). I just spent 172 on Biriyani. Am I still safe for the week?

Spending ₹172 on one meal is 34% of your total ₹500 budget. While the Biriyani was likely worth it, you now have only ₹328 left for the remaining days. Stick to 'Morning Maggie' (₹63) or Tea (₹24) for the next 2 days to re-balance your daily limit to ₹82/day.
I have INR 50 left and 2 days to go. Help!

CRITICAL AUSTERITY: You are in Strict Mode. With ₹50 left for 2 days, your limit is ₹25/day. This covers exactly one Juice (₹20) or one Tea (₹24). Bypassing all reserves—no more Biriyani until your next budget cycle.
Which category am I overspending on?

Looking at your history, your 'Meals' category is your biggest drain. You have frequent high-cost entries like Biriyani (₹172) and Ice Cream (₹25). If you pivot toward home-cooked Maggie (₹63) or simpler meals for 60% of your cycle, you could save ₹150+ per budget.
How can I save more this week?

You have several 'Drink' and 'Juice' entries (₹20-₹30 each). While small, these occur daily. Consolidating these small expenses or swapping them for office/home-made tea could free up ₹100 over 4 days, which is 20% of your total budget.
Am I on track for my budget?

You are currently 68% through your budget with 50% of your time remaining. You are slightly overspent. To get back on track, you need to reduce your daily spend from its current average of ₹125 down to ₹80 for the final 2 days.
